reasoning: President Yamandú Orsi of Uruguay has strong political alignment with Brazilian President Lula da Silva. On March 1, 2025, during Orsi's inauguration, Lula formally and publicly invited him to the BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro scheduled for July. Further, Orsi has shown strong interest in Uruguay joining the BRICS New Development Bank (NDB) following a December 2024 meeting with NDB Chair Dilma Rousseff. On March 3, 2025, Uruguayan newspaper El País reported affirmatively that Orsi "will attend" the summit, indicating early intent from his administration. Chancellor Mario Lubetkin also highlighted the invitation during his initial addresses. Given the clear intention, high political compatibility, and logistical ease (the summit being in nearby Rio), it is extremely likely Orsi will attend. Because standard diplomatic protocol and proactive local press coverage typically secure on-the-record confirmations of a highly-anticipated presidential trip weeks in advance, the probability of an official communication (written or oral) confirming his attendance before the July 1, 2025 deadline is high, though slight uncertainty remains about whether the formal travel announcement could be delayed until the start of July.
